data.title: First Industrial Realty Trust Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
First Industrial Realty Trust, Inc., a leading fully integrated owner, operator, and developer of logistics real estate, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company has demonstrated robust performance across various financial and operational metrics, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market positioning.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, First Industrial reported a diluted net income available to common stockholders per share (EPS) of $0.52, down from $0.67 in the same period last year. The full year 2024 EPS was $2.17, compared to $2.07 in 2023. The company's funds from operations (FFO) for the fourth quarter was $0.71 per share/unit on a diluted basis, up from $0.63 per share/unit a year ago. Full year 2024 FFO was $2.65 per share/unit, reflecting an 8.6% growth from $2.44 in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

First Industrial's in-service occupancy was 96.2% at the end of the fourth quarter, an improvement from 95.0% at the end of the third quarter and 95.5% at the end of the fourth quarter of 2023. The company signed 4.7 million square feet of development leases in 2024, the second highest annual total since 2012, with 1.4 million square feet signed in the fourth quarter alone. Cash rental rates increased by 50.8% for the full year, marking the second consecutive annual increase greater than 50%.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the fourth quarter, First Industrial commenced development of two projects totaling 679,000 square feet with an estimated investment of $96 million. The company also sold five buildings for $25 million in the fourth quarter, contributing to a total of $163 million in sales for the full year 2024. Additionally, the board of directors declared a common dividend of $0.445 per share/unit for the first quarter of 2025, representing a 20.3% increase from the prior rate.

Management's Perspective

Peter E. Baccile, president and chief executive officer of First Industrial, highlighted the company's strong portfolio operating metrics and development leasing efforts. He expressed gratitude to the regional teams for their performance and emphasized the broad-based nature of the development leasing across ten markets.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2025, First Industrial has initiated NAREIT FFO guidance at a range of $2.87 to $2.97 per share/unit, indicating approximately 10% growth at the midpoint. The company expects to deliver growth in FFO per share driven by completed and future development leasing and continuing strong operating performance. The average quarter-end in-service occupancy is projected to be between 95.0% and 96.0%, with SS NOI growth on a cash basis before termination fees expected to be between 6.0% and 7.0%.

SEC Filing: FIRST INDUSTRIAL REALTY TRUST INC [ FR ] - 8-K - Feb. 05, 2025

data.title: Brandywine Realty Trust Q4 and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Brandywine Realty Trust has released its financial and operating results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company, a prominent real estate investment trust (REIT) with a focus on the Philadelphia and Austin markets, has provided insights into its performance, strategic initiatives, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Brandywine Realty Trust reported a net loss available to common shareholders of $(43.3) million, or $(0.25) per share. This includes a non-cash impairment charge of $(23.8) million, primarily related to two unconsolidated joint ventures in the Metropolitan D.C. area. Funds from Operations (FFO) available to common shareholders stood at $29.9 million, or $0.17 per diluted share.

For the full year 2024, the company reported a net loss of $(195.6) million, or $(1.13) per share, compared to a net loss of $(197.4) million, or $(1.15) per share, in 2023. Full-year FFO available to common shareholders was $148.9 million, or $0.85 per diluted share, down from $198.3 million, or $1.15 per diluted share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Brandywine Realty Trust's core portfolio was 87.8% occupied and 89.9% leased as of December 31, 2024. The company signed new and renewal leases totaling 486,000 square feet in the fourth quarter and 1,306,000 square feet for the full year. The tenant retention ratio was 76% for the fourth quarter and 63% for the full year 2024.

On the transaction front, the company completed significant dispositions, including the sale of One and Two Barton Skyway in Austin for $107.6 million and its 50% interest in 4040 Wilson in Arlington for $190.5 million. These sales contributed to a strong liquidity position with no borrowings on the $600 million unsecured line of credit and $90 million cash-on-hand.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Brandywine Realty Trust completed over $300 million in dispositions, exceeding its initial $90 million target. The company also announced 783,000 square feet of commercial leasing, including 130,000 square feet within its developments commencing in 2026. The development projects, including the life science project at 3151 Market Street in Philadelphia, are now complete and in various phases of lease-up.

The Board of Trustees declared a quarterly dividend distribution of $0.15 per common share, paid on January 23, 2025, to shareholders of record as of January 8, 2025.

Management's Perspective

Jerry Sweeney, President and CEO of Brandywine Realty Trust, highlighted the company's achievements in meeting or exceeding its 2024 business plan objectives, including speculative revenue, tenant retention, and rental rate mark-to-markets. Sweeney emphasized the company's strong liquidity position and the completion of development projects, which are now in various phases of lease-up.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Brandywine Realty Trust has provided its 2025 guidance, projecting a loss per share of $(0.60) to $(0.48) and FFO per diluted share of $0.60 to $0.72. The company expects year-end core occupancy to range between 88-89% and core leased range between 89-90%. The guidance reflects increased expenses due to the expiration of capitalization periods on recently delivered developments and the expensing of returns on partners' preferred equity investments.

SEC Filing: BRANDYWINE REALTY TRUST [ BDN ] - 8-K - Feb. 05, 2025

Qualcomm shares fall on downbeat forecast for licensing business
data.description:
Qualcomm shares fell 4.2% in premarket trading on Thursday as the chipmaker's downbeat forecast for its patent licensing business overshadowed strong expectations for quarterly sales and profit.

The company disappointed investors after it said the licensing business, which accounted for 14.8% of its total revenue in the reported quarter, would see no sales growth this year following the expiration of its agreement with Huawei Technologies (HWT.UL).

Analysts at TD Cowen said they were expecting the impact of the removal of Huawei royalty to be mild but see it adding "another brick to the wall of worry facing the stock."

Qualcomm, the world's biggest supplier of modem chips that connect smartphones to wireless data networks, beat first-quarter results on strong demand for AI features in mobile devices.

The midpoint of the second-quarter sales forecast of $10.75 billion and an adjusted profit of $2.80 per share, came in above analysts' estimates of sales of $10.34 billion and an adjusted profit of $2.69 per share, according to data from LSEG.

Qualcomm credited robust sales from China for growth in its smartphone unit, as government subsidies and a slew of flagship launches power demand in the country.

While analysts at BofA Global Research expect demand trends to normalize in China in the long term, they said new handset subsidies could fuel demand in the near term.

Although the chip designer's shares rose modestly in 2024, their gains of 6% pale in comparison with a 171% jump in AI chip leader Nvidia's stock. They have, however, far outperformed shares of Intel , which lost 60%, and Advanced Micro Devices , which shed 18%, last year.

Qualcomm's median price target is at $194.5, compared with $199 before the results, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Qualcomm's 12-month forward price-to-earnings ratio is 15.02, compared with Nvidia's 27.64, INTC's 32.21 and AMD's 22.5.

Arm Holdings shares dropped 4.6% after the chip tech provider said it would no longer meet the top end of its previous full-year forecast. Qualcomm also said Arm has withdrawn a threat to terminate its license agreement with the chip technology provider.

Year-end report 2024
data.description:
Fabege

The operating surplus increased slightly, while interest costs were in line with the previous year. Unrealized value changes in the property portfolio turned slightly positive in the second half of the year. The profit before and after tax thus increased in comparison with the previous year.

October - December 2024

January - December 2024

  • Rental income increased to SEK 8616m (827).
  • Rental income increased to SEK 3,438m (3,366). On a like-for-like basis, rental income increased by just over 4,5 per cent (11).
  • Net operating income increased to SEK 625m (605).
  • Net operating income increased to SEK 2,552m (2,524). On a like-for-like basis, net operating income increased by 4,9 per cent (14).
  • The surplus ratio amounted to 73 per cent (76).
  • The surplus ratio amounted to 75 per cent (74).
  • Revenue from residential development amounted to SEK 3m (-67) and gross earnings totalled SEK -3m (-19).
  • Revenue from residential development amounted to SEK 233m (553) and gross earnings totalled SEK -21m (4).
  • Profit from property management amounted toSEK 333m (345).
  • Profit from property management amounted to SEK 1,345m (1,458).
  • Realised and unrealised changes in the value of properties amounted to SEK 18m (-2 415).
  • Realised and unrealised changes in the value of properties amounted to SEK -1,218m (-7,831)
  • Net lettings amounted to SEK -23m (168).
  • Net lettings amounted to SEK -108m (165).
  • Post-tax earnings for the year totalled SEK 455m(-1,991), corresponding to earnings per share of SEK 1:45 (-6:33)
  • Post-tax earnings for the year totalled SEK -89m(-7,831), corresponding to earnings per share of SEK -0:68 (-17:54)
  • The Board proposes a dividend of SEK 2.00 per share (1.80), to be paid quarterly in the amount of SEK 0.50 per share on each occasion (0.45)

 

-       After a tough 2024, we deliver a stable result with increased rental income and improved net operating income. The financial net also developed positively during the year. However, the management result decreased slightly and the net letting was disappointing. Our assessment today is that we will begin to see a turnaround in the economy in 2025, which will positively affect both the rental and transaction markets. We have faith in the future regarding Stockholm's office market, comments Stefan Dahlbo, CEO Fabege.

data.title: Fabege CEO Stefan Dahlbo to retire
data.description:
Fabege

Stefan Dahlbo, who has been CEO of Fabege since September 2019, has decided to retire by the end of 2025 at the latest.

-          "It's fantastic to have had the opportunity to work with Fabege and all its employees, customers, partners and owners. Although I still find the task enjoyable and inspiring, it is time to hand over to somebody else and do something different in my everyday life. The first half of the 2020s has involved a lot of challenges, but at the same time Fabege has continued to develop and is in a strong position for the future," says Stefan Dahlbo CEO.

-      "I would like to thank Stefan for everything he has done for the company during his years as CEO and for the responsibility he has taken in recent years when the property industry has been a turbulent environment in which to work," says Jan Litborn, Chair of the Board of Fabege.

The board has hired Amrop AB to help with the recruitment of a new CEO:

Fabege AB (publ)

This information is of the type that Fabege AB is required to disclose under the EU Market Abuse Regulation. This information was released, through the provision of the above-mentioned contact person, for publication  on 6 February 2025, at 12.00 pm CET.

For further information, please contact:

Jan Litborn, Chair of the Board, Fabege AB, tel. +46 (0)70 563 88 00

Stefan Dahlbo, President and CEO, tel. +46 (0)70 353 18 88, stefan.dahlbo@fabege.se

data.title: BenevolentAI to Seek Shareholder Approval on Amsterdam Delisting, Merger with Osaka Holdings
data.description:
BenevolentAI scheduled a March 12 meeting for shareholders to vote on its proposed delisting from Euronext Amsterdam through a merger with Osaka Holdings.

The artificial intelligence biopharma drug company said Thursday the move would cut the costs related to the listing and free up capital for its core business activities.

The merger is expected to be completed on March 12, with the delisting scheduled for March 13, subject to shareholder approval.

data.title: Dutch bank ING's 2024 Net Profit Takes a Hit From Higher Costs, Loan Provisions
data.description:
ING Groep booked a 12.3% year-over-year fall in net result for full-year 2024, dragged down by a higher addition to loan loss provisions and an increase in operating expenses.

The Netherlands-based banking group's net result for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, fell to 6.39 billion euros from the year-ago 7.29 billion euros, according to a Thursday release. The full-year figure was also dented by a 25.9% year-over-year drop in fourth-quarter net result to 1.15 billion euros.

Operating expenses in 2024 increased 4.8% to 12.12 billion euros, which the group mainly attributed to the impact of inflation on staff expenses, and net additions to loan loss provisions more than doubled to 1.19 billion euros. Total income, however, rose to a record 22.62 billion euros over the period from 22.58 billion euros, despite a decline in net interest income.

"Overall, we consider ING's 4Q24 results ok to slightly soft. The bank missed 4Q24 pre-provision profit by 3% due to higher costs (though partially explained by restructuring charge) and the quality of the NII beat was not ideal," analysts at Deutsche Bank Research said.

Meanwhile, ING's board proposed a final cash dividend of 0.71 euro per share, compared with 0.756 euro per share distributed a year before.

For 2025, ING expects total income to be roughly in line with 2024, bolstered by volume growth and a targeted 5% to 10% increase in fee income. Total expenses are projected to rise to between 12.5 billion euros and 12.7 billion euros, with the common equity Tier 1 ratio anticipated to converge toward a target of 12.5% by 2025-end.

Analysts at Deutsche Bank noted that the new guidance "is modestly below consensus as 2025 revenues are ahead of consensus, but so are costs."

"For the coming year, we remain vigilant as we foresee ongoing geopolitical volatility and a fragmented economic outlook," said ING Chief Executive Steven van Rijswijk.

As of late Thursday morning, ING shares were down more than 2% in Amsterdam.

data.title: Qualcomm Confirms Arm Holdings Drops Threat to End License Agreement
data.description:
Qualcomm said Wednesday that Arm Holdings has retracted its October 2024 notice to cancel its license.

The company's CEO, Cristiano Amon, told analysts during its Q1 earnings call that the architecture license agreement tied to a dispute over Qualcomm's computer chips will no longer be terminated.

The company's shares were down more than 5% in recent Thursday premarket activity.

China Overseas Land & Investment's January Contracted Sales Rise 14%
data.description:
China Overseas Land & Investment recorded contracted property sales of roughly 12 billion yuan in January, up 14% from a year prior, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Sales area stood at 593,100 square meters, an increase of 5.8% year-over-year.

As of Jan. 31, subscribed property sales stood at approximately 6.14 billion yuan.

In January, the property company acquired two land parcels in Beijing and Shenzhen with an attributable gross floor area of around 255,235 square meters.

data.title: Market Chatter: Boeing Company Sees Indian, South Asian Airlines Adding 2,835 Jets Over 20 Years
data.description:
Boeing expects Indian and South Asian airlines to add 2,835 commercial aircraft to their fleet over the next 20 years, Reuters reported Thursday, citing the US plane maker.

The company estimated that carriers in the two regions will take delivery of 2,445 single-aisle aircraft and 370 widebody aircraft.

The anticipated addition represents a four-fold increase over current levels, according to the report.

Boeing's previous rolling 20-year market forecast, issued last year, was for 2,705 jets, Reuters said.

Boeing did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment on the matter.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Increase 8.7 Million Barrels in Week Ended Jan. 31, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. crude oil inventories climbed 8.7 million barrels to 423.8 million barrels in the week ended Jan. 31, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its This Week in Petroleum report released Wednesday.

Crude oil production decreased by 21,000 barrels per day (b/d) to 13.42 million barrels week over week, the EIA reported.

Crude oil imports rose by 121,000 b/d to 6.56 million b/d while crude oil exports surged by 314,000 b/d to 4.15 million b/d, according to the agency.

Total gasoline stocks also jumped 2.2 million barrels to 251.1 million barrels. Distillate stockpiles, meanwhile, fell 5.5 million barrels to 118.5 million barrels, the EIA said.

data.title: AstraZeneca Expects Revenue, Earnings Growth Despite Sales Drop in China — Update
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

AstraZeneca said it forecasts sales and earnings growth this year after fourth-quarter results beat expectations, as strength in its oncology business offset weak China sales, but warned of a potential fine from a drug-import probe in the country.

The results lifted AstraZeneca's shares, which rose as much as 5% in European morning trade.

AstraZeneca said it could face a fine of up to $4.5 million if it is found liable in a probe into illegal drug-import allegations in China. The company had previously said current and former employees in China were under investigation and vowed to cooperate with authorities.

The company said Shenzhen's customs office notified it of suspected unpaid import taxes of $900,000 and that it might get a fine of between one and five times that amount. AstraZeneca said it believes the taxes relate to its Imfinzi and Imjudo cancer drugs, but Chief Executive Pascal Soriot said in a conference call the probe could possibly involve more drugs.

Looking ahead, AstraZeneca forecasts revenue would increase by a high single-digit percentage in 2025, with core earnings per share rising at a low double-digit percentage, when excluding currency movements.

AstraZeneca said core earnings per share--its preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--were $2.09 for the three months ended Dec. 31, up from $1.45 for the comparable period a year earlier. This compared with estimates of $2.07 taken from a company-compiled consensus.

After-tax profit rose to $1.50 billion from $959 million.

Total revenue for the quarter was $14.89 billion, ahead of expectations of $14.2 billion, and up from $12.02 billion the year before.

AstraZeneca's sales in China fell 1% to $1.36 billion. The company attributed the decline to low rates of seasonal respiratory viral infections and year-end hospital dynamics.

Chief Financial Officer Aradhana Sarin in January warned the company's China business could suffer a revenue impact in the last quarter of the year and going into this year amid the probe.

Investors' greatest concern had been the impact of the China investigation on the business, which seems manageable so far and appears to be progressing toward an eventual resolution, Barclays analysts said in a note to clients.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com
